title: kittui-wm v2: backend-agnostic compositor (local/remote/Mac/X11 windows as homogeneous kittui chrome)
description: Generalise kittui-wm so any window — local X11, remote X11 over SSH, native macOS via Quartz capture, XQuartz-hosted X11, future Wayland, future Tendril — appears in the user's terminal as the same kittui chrome surface. The compositor multiplexes multiple XServer backends behind one operator UI, with globally-unique window ids, per-window WindowMode (Floating or Tiled), per-window chrome theme, and a single hit-test/route_pointer/route_key pipeline. v2 must let the operator mix Mac windows (Quartz CGWindowList capture) with XQuartz X11 windows in the same session, all rendered with locally-composed kittui chrome.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Set q=2 quiet flag on all kitty control commands
description:
kitty emits diagnostic responses for upload, animation, placement, and delete commands unless q=1 (suppress OK responses) or q=2 (suppress all responses including errors) is set. The current encoder never sets q, which leaks Gi=<id>;ENOENT and OK lines into the terminal output and breaks the showcase visually. Wire a per-Runtime quiet mode (default q=2) and surface it via TerminalInfo / Runtime builder; preserve a verbose path for debugging.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Move kittui_wm_demo example to use kittui_cli::session::run_loop
description: The session loop has been extracted into kittui_cli::session for the new kitwm binary (bd-c60dd8). The kittui_wm_demo example still has its own copy. Replace the example's run_loop with a call to kittui_cli::session::run_loop and delete the duplicated raw-mode + debugger code from the example. Reduces example to ~80 lines of backend setup.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --serve binds Unix socket + --attach connects (minimal protocol)
description: Minimal daemon/client v1 for bd-fb5d9d/bd-aaea73. kitwm --serve binds $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (default /tmp/kitwm-0.sock) and accepts one client at a time. kitwm --attach connects, sends a Hello, receives a Welcome, runs a passthrough where stdin from the client goes to the daemon and the daemon's render bytes come back. Backend hosted by the daemon; client is a renderer only. Parent: bd-fb5d9d/bd-aaea73.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: SSH operator walkthrough
description: docs/operator-walkthrough.md: step-by-step for the SSH-into-Mac use case. Includes generating an SSH key for kittui-wm, enabling Screen Recording for sshd, setting KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY, attaching, detaching, killing the session. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: deliberately slow backend cannot stall UI thread
description: Add a SlowFakeServer that sleeps 500ms in capture(). Attach two backends to a MultiCompositor: SlowFakeServer + FakeServer. Assert that tick() returns within 33ms regardless of the slow backend's state, and that the slow backend's window eventually shows a non-empty frame after its first capture completes. This is the regression test for the pump architecture. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kitty spec deviations and conformance status
description:
Add a docs/protocol-conformance.md (or DESIGN.md section) that enumerates every section of https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ and marks kittui's status as supported / partial / unsupported, with links to the bead tracking any partial/unsupported items. Update README.md status line once the P0/P1 children of this epic land.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: End-to-end SSH demo: serve here, attach from another terminal
description: Practical demo + script: launch kittui-wm serve on host A, ssh from host B with KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:0 forwarded, run kittui-wm attach. Confirm Quartz capture pixels travel over SSH and pointer events route back. Capture screenshots from both terminals to prove same-session-different-viewers. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Make the wgpu backend a production-quality library backend
description: Operator direction: use the cacophony project as inspiration for canonical wgpu setup examples, but do not cargo-cult it because GPU support there may not have worked reliably. Treat kittui as a well-written library: design a robust wgpu backend abstraction with explicit adapter/device lifecycle, headless/offscreen compatibility on macOS/Linux, deterministic CPU fallback, clear errors, resource reuse across frames, no hidden global state, parity tests against CPU for supported nodes, and documentation of unsupported GPU features. This should refine/coordinate with bd-6c1165 (probe cache) and the renderer semantic beads rather than leaving GPU as a best-effort stub.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement Transport::File and Transport::Memory upload paths
description:
Transport enum currently advertises File and Memory but encode_chunked always emits a=t,f=100 streaming base64. Per spec t= selects the transmission medium (d direct, f file, t temp file, s shared memory) and the payload becomes a path or shm name instead of pixel bytes. Implement real File (write PNG to a temp path, send t=f with path) and Memory (POSIX shm or anonymous mmap, send t=s) transports, with cleanup, error handling, and fallbacks. Add tests and a CLI flag to select transport.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Triple-Ctrl-C exit guard; single Ctrl-C passes through
description: kittui-wm-demo (and the attach client) treat single Ctrl-C as an InputEvent::Char{ch: '\x03'} forwarded to the focused window. Track a counter that decays 1s after the last Ctrl-C; on count >= 3 within the window, restore terminal and exit. The 'q to quit' message in the footer changes to 'Ctrl-C Ctrl-C Ctrl-C to quit' once any backend is hosting an app that might consume Ctrl-C. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: kittui-wm hosts an xterm via XQuartz on macOS
description: Integration test that skips if XQuartz isn't installed. Spawns XquartzServer, launches xterm against the display, waits for it to map, calls compose() and asserts at least one non-empty capture is returned. The demo gains --features xquartz to drive this end-to-end with tendril screenshot proof. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs/wm.md: kittui-wm v1 architecture and operator guide
description:
Long-form doc covering: kittui-wm v1 architecture, how Xvfb is hosted, how pointer + key events flow, the compositor lifecycle, perf expectations, the WSL/Linux requirements, and the SSH operator story. Linked from README and DESIGN.md.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cache tests flake when run in parallel
description: cargo test -p kittui-cache --lib intermittently fails 2/8 when run alongside other crate tests, but passes deterministically with --test-threads=1 or in isolation. Likely temp-dir race in still_round_trip / animation_round_trip. File deterministic test fixtures with unique temp dirs per test.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete kitty graphics protocol conformance + ratakittui coverage
description: Operator-confirmed gap: kittui was supposed to be a careful extraction of the Cacophony TUI graphics lib against https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ but the showcase reproduces ENOENT image-not-found responses in Ghostty and produces only placeholder character grids. Audit shows multiple spec deviations: (1) unicode placeholders emit bare U+10EEEE with foreground color only, missing the (row, column, msb-of-image-id) combining diacritics that the spec requires for cell-to-pixel anchoring; (2) the q= quiet field is never set so kitty prints diagnostic responses inline; (3) declared Transport::File and Transport::Memory never adjust t= and still base64-stream via a=t; (4) animation upload uses a=t with r= instead of a=t for first frame then a=f for subsequent frames and the animation control verb shape is wrong; (5) placement command lacks p= placement-id support and X=/Y= subcell offset support; (6) tests assert substrings of the current malformed output instead of the protocol grammar. This epic tracks getting kittui to a verified spec-conformant baseline plus full ratakittui widget coverage proof. Child beads will land incrementally with grammar-pinned tests and a kittui proof CLI mode the operator can paste into Ghostty/kitty for visual confirmation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-quartz: macOS virtual-display backend (CGVirtualDisplay + ScreenCaptureKit + CGEventPost)
description: Add a sibling crate kittui-quartz that implements the kittui-xvfb XServer trait against macOS Quartz. Creates a CGVirtualDisplay via the private CoreGraphics symbols (only macOS path to a real headless display without third-party drivers), captures it with ScreenCaptureKit, and routes pointer/key events via CGEventPost + Accessibility. Gated behind a 'quartz' feature; documents the private-API trade-off in docs/wm.md. Lands the XServer trait implementation now; the CGVirtualDisplayCreate FFI itself behind unsafe extern "C" symbols, with a graceful fallback to capturing the main display when virtual display creation fails. The kittui_wm_demo learns --features quartz to drive it on macOS the same way --features xvfb drives Xvfb on Linux.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-input: parse kitty mouse + key reporting
description:
New crate kittui-input that parses the kitty pointer protocol (SGR mouse 1006, motion 1003, focus 1004, hover 1006 with extensions) and CSI key reports into typed events. Includes subcell pixel coordinates where the host reports them. Exact-grammar tests for press/release/motion/scroll/drag/hover and modifier flags.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Demo example: kittui-wm-demo launches Xvfb + an X app
description:
crates/kittui-cli/examples/kittui_wm_demo.rs that starts Xvfb at :99, launches xterm (or any X app), composites it as a floating kittui window in the current terminal with full pointer/keyboard routing, and shows the perf panel beside it. Documents the SSH-over-kittui story in docs/wm.md.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix animation upload to use a=t/a=f frame appends and a=a control
description:
Animation upload currently uses repeated a=t,r=N which the spec does not document for appending frames. Per https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#animation the first frame uses a=t with f= and i= as usual, subsequent frames use a=f with the same i= to append, and the animation control sets gap/loop count via a=a with parameters s=, c=, z=. Audit kittui-kitty::upload_animation against the spec, fix the verb sequence, set per-frame delays via the appropriate field, add grammar tests, and ensure native looping plays without escape traffic per cycle.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Compositor multiplex: many backends, one session
description:
Generalise kittui-wm::compositor::Compositor from Compositor<S: XServer> to Compositor holding Vec<Box<dyn XServer>>. Introduce a globally-unique WindowKey = (backend_id, XWindowId) so two backends can't collide. Update hit_test, route_pointer, route_key, compose to dispatch by backend. Per-window mode + chrome theme still live in the compositor. All existing tests must keep passing.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs/wm.md v2: multi-backend mental model + operator workflow
description:
Rewrite docs/wm.md to lead with the multi-backend mental model: any window is local/remote/Quartz/XQuartz, rendered as homogenous kittui chrome. Include backend matrix from the design conversation, the SSH-into-Mac story, the mixed X11+macOS session example, and the limits (no Cocoa hosting without v3 CGVirtualDisplay).

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: docs wm dot md v two rewrite

title: Add fuzz targets and workspace integration smoke from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md calls for cargo-fuzz targets for Scene::from_json and CLI flag parsers plus a workspace integration smoke test that builds a runtime, renders a small scene, verifies PNG bytes, and checks placement escapes. Current repo has unit/golden/parity/ABI tests, but no fuzz/ directory and no root tests/it.rs smoke. Add bounded fuzz targets and a fast integration smoke that can run in CI without a real kitty terminal.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: add fuzz targets and integration tests

title: kittui-wm Compositor + Floating layout + LifecycleTracker integration
description:
Add Compositor type owning a kittui::Runtime, LifecycleTracker, and a window-id -> placement-id map. Adds LayoutNode::Floating { rect_px, draggable } and a per-frame composite() that turns Xvfb window snapshots into Scene::Image placements with chrome (border, glow, scanlines from the existing ratakittui design), then drains the LifecycleTracker so closed X windows emit kitty deletes.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add nix flake
description: Add a nix flake to the project. See tendril and other rust caco projects as examples for implementation details and best practices.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kitwm record: dump N capture frames to PNG sequence for debugging
description: kitwm record [--frames N] [--out DIR] runs the chosen backend for N frames, writes each capture as PNG into DIR (default /tmp/kitwm-record-<timestamp>/), prints the directory path. Useful for: visually verifying the capture pipeline without TCC permissions on the WM terminal; bug reports; making demo gifs. Works with --capture SPEC. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Tiling layout integration: split/stack/tab + floating mix
description:
Extend LayoutNode to allow Floating windows sitting on top of a tiled tree. Compositor renders the tiled tree first (one Scene::Image per pane), then Floating windows with z-order. Add resize and move keybindings (Mod+arrow / Mod+drag) routed through the Compositor.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm: flicker between frames — drop \x1b[H\x1b[J + only re-emit changed windows
description: Operator: 'image flickers between frames. should be a smooth consistent WM experience, only insides of windows are latency bound but can't cause main WM to flicker'. Cause: session::run_loop writes \x1b[H\x1b[J every frame to clear, then re-emits each window's upload+placement+placeholder. The clear wipes the previous frame before the new pixels arrive over the slow tmux passthrough, so the operator sees a black flash per frame. Fix: stop clearing the screen, kitty atomically replaces an image at the same id; only emit placement+placeholder on first frame per window or when footprint changes; never re-emit when nothing changed; track last-seen window set so removed windows get a single delete escape. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: WM-thread architecture: per-backend Pump (capture+encode), non-blocking UI tick
description: Move capture + PNG/raw-RGB encode off the UI thread. Each attached backend becomes a Pump with its own worker thread that loops capture -> encode -> store-in-slot. MultiCompositor::tick(layout) reads slots non-blockingly and returns whatever's there. UI thread keeps running input, resize, layout, hit-test at 60fps regardless of slow windows. Slow windows just show their previous frame until they catch up.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description: Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process ("server") and a thin viewer process ("client"), communicating over a Unix domain socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux's $TMUX or X11's $DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux's pane sharing) — useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock).\n\nMakes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Raw RGB transmission via kitty f=32 in kittui-kitty
description: upload_still_ex grows a CaptureFormat option: Png (current default) or Rgba32 (f=32). When Rgba32, skip PNG encode and emit raw RGBA bytes base64'd directly. Update kittui::Runtime::place to accept a CaptureFormat hint on Scene or via a RuntimeBuilder option. Add an exact-grammar test that asserts the f=32 path emits a=t,f=32,s=W,v=H,m=0,q=2 followed by base64 RGBA. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement deterministic Scene normalization before hashing
description: DESIGN.md says Scene::id() must hash a deterministic normalized scene: drop empty/zero-opacity layers, flatten normal composites, snap pixel rects to 1/64 px, clamp/sort gradient stops, and remove zero-width/transparent structural no-ops. Current crates/kittui-core/src/scene.rs hashes the raw serde form directly, so semantically equivalent scenes can miss the cache and produce different kitty ids. Add a normalizer in kittui-core, test id stability/equivalence with property or fixture tests, and keep serde JSON compatibility stable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Operator UI: live backend picker + window picker in kittui-wm
description:
Inside the running kittui-wm session, support hotkeys to spawn/attach backends (local Xvfb, XQuartz, Quartz capture target) and pick which windows to surface. Use the ratakittui design language: floating side panel listing 'available backends' and 'live windows' with arrow keys to navigate, Enter to surface, Mod+T to toggle Tiled/Floating per window.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add renderer probe cache and GPU parity gating to Runtime Auto mode
description: DESIGN.md says Runtime Auto should try GPU once, run a CPU/GPU parity fixture with SSIM tolerance, persist gpu_status/gpu_adapter/gpu_parity_ssim in <cache>/probe.json, and skip future live probes until invalidated. kittui-cache has a ProbeRecord helper, but Runtime currently only lazily initializes GPU and permanently falls back within the process without parity checking or persisted probe decisions. Wire probe.json into RuntimeBuilder/Auto mode, implement probe --force invalidation, and test fallback behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: ratakittui: complete widget coverage example + decoration matrix
description:
crates/kittui-cli/examples/ratatui_showcase.rs and ratakittui currently expose a wide widget surface (Block, Paragraph, List, Table, Tabs, Gauge, Sparkline, LineGauge, BarChart, Chart, Canvas, Scrollbar, Clear) plus inline (Chip, Title, Divider, Line). Once the protocol fixes land, expand the ratatui showcase to render every wrapper with assistant/tool/user tones, animated vs static chrome, JoinGroup adjacency, and the LifecycleTracker delete pass. Add at least one snapshot or grammar test per wrapper that pins the produced Scene id and the placement footprint.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Pump: per-backend worker thread for capture+encode
description: Land kittui-wm::compositor::Pump. Each attached backend gets a worker thread looping: backend.windows() -> for each window, capture() -> store in lock-free slot keyed by WindowKey. MultiCompositor::tick(layout) reads slots non-blockingly; missing slots show last-known frame. SharedServer wraps Arc<dyn XServer> so a backend can be shared between the synchronous path and the pump. Update MultiCompositor::attach_async to spawn a Pump. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Encode kitty unicode-placeholder diacritics per spec
description:
placeholder_text in crates/kittui-kitty currently emits bare U+10EEEE characters with only a \x1b[38:2:r:g:b]m foreground color set to the low 24 bits of the image id. The kitty graphics spec (https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#unicode-placeholders) requires every placeholder cell to carry up to three combining diacritics encoding (row, column, most-significant-byte-of-image-id). Without these, kitty/Ghostty treat every cell as row 0 column 0 of the image and the placement does not anchor. Implement diacritic encoding from the spec's tables, support image ids whose top byte is non-zero, add exact-grammar unit tests, and snapshot the placeholder grid for a known fixture.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-xquartz: XQuartz-Xvfb backend for X11 on macOS
description:
New crate kittui-xquartz that spawns XQuartz in Xvfb mode (Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac) and connects via x11rb the same way kittui-xvfb::xvfb::XvfbServer does. Implements the XServer trait so the compositor sees XQuartz the same as any other backend. Documents the XQuartz install path (/opt/X11/bin/Xquartz, or via brew install --cask xquartz).

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: macOS quickstart with XQuartz install + Nix integration
description: Update docs/wm.md macOS section: brew install --cask xquartz, then cargo run --release -p kittui-cli --example kittui_wm_demo --features quartz,xquartz. Include a Nix fragment that adds /opt/X11/bin to PATH for the devshell on Darwin. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Showcase: cover every protocol mode end-to-end
description:
Rewrite crates/kittui-cli/examples/showcase.rs to walk the full protocol surface: Direct + Tmux + File + Memory transports; still + animated scenes; unicode-placeholder + absolute placement; multiple placements of the same scene; subcell offsets; delete + replace lifecycle; HiDPI cell-size override. Each section should be labelled and visually distinct in Ghostty/kitty. Verify against the kitty spec sections it exercises.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Multi-display enumeration in QuartzServer
description:
Add QuartzServer::displays() returning every connected CGDirectDisplayID with its CGRect bounds, scale factor, and a friendly name. Compositor exposes a way to spawn a capture target per display. Useful when the operator's Mac has multiple monitors and they want to mirror only one.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: latency + bandwidth under SSH
description: Measure round-trip latency (input event to next frame back) over loopback SSH; document in docs/wm.md. Set a soft target of <200ms RTT on a same-machine SSH session. File a perf bead if exceeded. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete terminal transport probing and File/Memory transfer modes
description: DESIGN.md specifies TerminalInfo::detect(), automatic tmux detection, and kitty File/Memory transports (`a=t,t=f` and `a=t,t=s`) in addition to Direct/TmuxPassthrough. Current TerminalInfo only provides defaults/overrides, and kittui-kitty treats File/Memory like direct chunked base64. Implement environment probing over TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/KITTY_WINDOW_ID/KITTY_PUBLIC_KEY/TMUX/WT_SESSION, CLI transport override, file-transfer emission, shared-memory transfer where supported with safe fallback, and tests for all transport encodings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm v1: Xvfb-backed WM rendering app windows as floating/tiling chrome
description: Operator direction: kittui-wm becomes a real terminal window manager that hosts Xvfb (or per-app Xephyr) in the agent process tree, captures each X window's framebuffer through SHM, composites them as floating or tiling kittui chrome inside the user's terminal, and routes kitty pointer/keyboard events back to the X server via XTestFake* so the hosted apps respond to clicks and keys. This is the substrate that turns kittui into a full WM over SSH: PATH inherits, env inherits, so anything spawned inside the nested X session picks up the operator's environment. v1 scope: (1) kittui-input parses kitty's mouse + key reporting; (2) kittui-xvfb spawns Xvfb, claims a DISPLAY, captures the root window via MIT-SHM and exposes per-window rects; (3) kittui-wm gains a Compositor that owns a kittui::Runtime + LifecycleTracker, places one Image scene per X window, and feeds pointer events back through XTest; (4) tiling and floating layouts implemented on top of LayoutNode::{Split,Stack,Tab,Floating}; (5) demo example that launches Xvfb + a small X app and renders it as a kittui window. Out of scope for v1: Wayland support, GPU passthrough, raw RGB(A) transmission, multi-display.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: App launcher inside kittui-wm: a kittui floating menu to spawn apps
description: Hotkey (Mod+Return by default) opens a kittui floating launcher: a text input + matching app list pulled from common locations ($PATH binaries via 'compgen -c' equivalent, /Applications/*.app, /opt/homebrew/bin, /nix/store/mlrgd5zljyrxl15ryn31w15w48f6yii5-cargo-1.94.1/share:/nix/store/c9vv24f9bdnhnms10nwijn6q8wjz00ak-just-1.51.0/share:/nix/store/cn0f42d8p48g718nh2zsgl96zjx207hi-nixfmt-1.2.0/share:/nix/store/7693fhfqg2hddwl3kbbpinrdqdp71nq2-pkg-config-wrapper-0.29.2/share:/nix/store/xvmhkpvfvmy4sfdkqwg9inq3qkpnx81b-libiconv-109.100.2/share:/Users/harryaskham/.nix-profile/share:/run/current-system/sw/share:/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/share/applications). Selecting an entry spawns the app under the currently-active backend (Xvfb/XQuartz for X apps; Quartz capture mode for native Mac apps). v1 supports launching X apps inside XQuartz. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Pointer + key routing back into Xvfb via XTestFake*
description:
Route kittui-input events back to the active Xvfb-hosted window. Mouse hit-tests pick the topmost LayoutNode::Floating or tiling pane under the cursor and translate the local pixel coordinate into XTestFakeMotionEvent/XTestFakeButtonEvent; keys go through XTestFakeKeyEvent with the host's keymap. Mod state preserved across events.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --capture window:<substr> / display:<n> / main / all (scriptable target)
description: Add a --capture flag that takes a target spec: 'window:<title-substr>' picks the first CGWindow whose owner_name or title contains the substring; 'display:<n>' picks the nth connected display (0=main); 'main' (default) main display; 'all' all displays. Lets the operator launch kitwm against a specific source without the interactive picker, e.g. 'kitwm --capture window:Safari' or 'kitwm --capture display:1'. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: picker selection visibly surfaces a chosen window
description: Drive the demo with a FakeServer holding 3 named windows; programmatically send Mod+Space, navigate to window 2, press Enter. Assert MultiCompositor now has window 2 attached as a Tiled backend and its capture is included in compose() output. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description: Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process (server) and a thin viewer process (client), communicating over a Unix domain socket at KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux TMUX or X11 DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux pane sharing) - useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock). Makes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: picker keybindings + screenshot in docs/wm.md
description: Document the Mod+Space picker, Mod+W cycle, Mod+1..9 quick-select. Capture a screenshot of the picker open in the kittui terminal. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement real Mask, Clip, and BlendMode rendering semantics
description: DESIGN.md includes Node::Composite with BlendMode::{Normal, Add, Multiply, Screen}, Node::Mask, and Node::Clip. Current CPU rasterizer passes through Composite children using normal source-over and renders Mask/Clip children without applying mask alpha or clip bounds; GPU encode similarly ignores the blend/mask/clip semantics. Implement matching CPU/GPU semantics or explicit CPU-first semantics with golden tests for add/multiply/screen, masked content, and clipped content.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement user Shader node rendering and pipeline cache
description: DESIGN.md/Future ideas mention Node::Shader with per-scene WGSL pipeline compilation, caching, and GPU execution, while CPU should return a clear GPU-only error. Current GPU encode accepts Shader in the type system but draws nothing, and CPU returns UnsupportedImage. Implement shader validation, uniform binding, per-scene pipeline cache, error reporting, and tests that Shader nodes either render through GPU or fail clearly under CPU-only mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix kitty upload chunk syntax causing Ghostty ENOENT in showcase
description: Harry reported cargo run -p kittui-cli --example showcase in Ghostty prints placeholder glyphs plus kitty responses like Gi=<id>;ENOENT: image not found. Inspection found kittui-kitty encode_chunked emits upload commands as ESC_G,a=t,... with a leading comma before a=t; placement commands use ESC_Ga=p... and are parsed, so Ghostty reports placements for images whose uploads were ignored. Fix upload chunk control syntax, add regression coverage, and verify kittui-kitty tests. Filed from operator report.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Release prep: README v0.3 + DESIGN.md update + version bump
description: README leads with kitwm + a 30-second-quickstart. DESIGN.md gets a kittui-wm v2 section reflecting the daemon/client model. Workspace version 0.3.0. CHANGELOG entry summarising the v2 epic landings. Tag release once all P0/P1 children of bd-031e54 close. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: kitwm with no args opens a usable session, launches xterm, you can type into it
description: Integration: kitwm starts, app launcher opens via Mod+Return, type 'xterm', press Enter, xterm appears in the kittui terminal, type 'echo hello' inside it, the chars route through, the output is captured back. Final visual proof via tendril screenshot. This is the v2 acceptance criterion. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm attach: thin viewer client
description: kittui-wm attach (or kittui-wm with no args + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY set) connects to the daemon socket and runs the existing run_loop as a renderer: receives scene snapshots from CmdTick replies, posts InputEvents on stdin parse, never touches backends directly. Multiple clients can attach to the same session simultaneously. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Finish v1 CLI surface from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md lists a broader CLI than currently implemented: panel, image, place, scene emit, cache info/gc/clear, probe --force, --transport, --renderer, --upload-only/--placement-only/--embed-only, --dry-run, pixel and percentage sizing, richer colors, glow/scanline/shadow/label options, and animation curve validation. Some cache commands exist, but most common flags and subcommands do not. Fill out the v1 CLI surface in thin wrappers over Runtime/Composition and add integration tests for JSON/dry-run/raw output modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: daemon/client architecture in docs/wm.md
description: Long-form description of the daemon/client split: protocol, socket lifecycle, multi-client semantics, security (file permissions on the socket), comparison to tmux's server. Operator quickstart: kittui-wm serve & ; KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:0 kittui-wm attach. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: protocol-conformance.md row for f=32 raw RGB
description: Add f=32 to the spec coverage table in docs/protocol-conformance.md. Mark as 'supported' once the kittui-kitty test and the demo measurement both land. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: WM-thread model + pump lifecycle in docs/wm.md
description: Add a section to docs/wm.md describing the UI thread vs Pump worker thread split, when frames go stale, how backpressure works (latest-frame-wins). Include a small ASCII diagram. Add a perf table updated with measured frame times from the demo against the FakeServer + Quartz pump. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: detach + reattach preserves session state
description: Integration test: spawn daemon with a FakeServer backend, attach client A, send pointer events, detach. Spawn client B (new process), attach to same socket, verify it sees the same session state (windows, focus, layout) immediately. Asserts the daemon outlived A and B starts at the same state, not a fresh one. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix macOS kittui-cli link failure for missing libiconv
description: While validating bd-c3214b on ms-mac, cargo build -p kittui-cli and cargo test -p kittui-cli --bins compiled successfully but failed at link time with ld: library not found for -liconv. cargo check -p kittui-cli passes, so this appears to be host/toolchain linker configuration or an existing native dependency issue rather than a source compile error from the config change. Command evidence: cargo build -p kittui-cli -> cc ... -liconv ... ld: library not found for -liconv. Filed from bd-c3214b validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Wire f=32 into kittui-wm-demo + measure speedup
description: kittui-wm-demo with --features sck sets the Runtime to Rgba32. Measure frame budget from /tmp/kittui-wm.log before and after. Expectation: budget drops from ~60ms to <16ms on the M2. Capture screenshot via tendril of the demo running at smooth FPS. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Backend manifest spec + persistent session file
description:
Add a session file format (~/.config/kittui-wm/session.toml) describing which backends to spawn on startup, their config, default layouts, per-window chrome themes. kittui-wm loads it at startup; the operator picker writes back to it. Means the operator can configure 'on launch, attach Quartz to display 1, spawn XQuartz at :99 with xterm tiled top-left, mirror Safari floating bottom-right'.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add layered kittui configuration resolution
description: Operator direction: every user-facing kittui option should be settable via explicit CLI/API flag, KITTUI_* environment variable, or ~/.config/kittui/config.yaml, with precedence flag/API override > env override > YAML default. This lets scripts establish an env/config scope and call kittui many times or batch operations without repeating every flag, while apps can still force overrides. Design and implement a typed Config model, YAML loader (respecting XDG_CONFIG_HOME), KITTUI_* env parsing, source/precedence diagnostics for --json output, validation/error messages, and tests covering precedence and malformed config.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Triple-Ctrl-C exit guard; otherwise forward Ctrl-C to focused window
description: kittui-wm-demo should treat a single Ctrl-C as a passthrough to the focused window (since hosted apps may need it), and only exit on three Ctrl-C presses within a 1s decaying window. Add a tiny counter that decays after 1s and only triggers RawMode::drop + exit on count >= 3.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm: single-command launcher that spawns daemon + attaches
description: Add a top-level bin 'kitwm' (or 'kittyui-wm') in kittui-cli that, when invoked with no args, does: 1. detect KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY or pick a free socket, 2. check whether a daemon is already listening; if yes attach to it, 3. if no, fork a daemon process that survives the parent and attach to it. With --serve, only run daemon; with --attach, only attach; with --kill, signal daemon shutdown. This is the user-facing entrypoint. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: Ctrl-C delivery + triple-quit
description: Integration test: drive a FakeServer that records key events. Send one InputEvent containing Ctrl-C, assert FakeServer received it. Send three Ctrl-C events within 500ms, assert run_loop returns. Send three Ctrl-C with 1.5s gaps between each, assert run_loop does NOT return and FakeServer received three Ctrl-Cs. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement Image node rasterization and kittui image CLI
description: DESIGN.md defines Node::Image plus CLI `kittui image --src PATH -w W -h H --fit ... --tint ...`. Current CPU renderer returns UnsupportedImage for Node::Image and GPU encode drops image nodes; the CLI has no image subcommand. Implement PNG/JPEG image decoding behind the intended feature split, shared Fit geometry, CPU raster copy/tint path, GPU texture/atlas or documented CPU fallback path, image cache storage, and CLI coverage with tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Surface project config validation blockers in caco commands
description: While finishing bd-5c2b47 in the kittui project, every caco surface I tried (`caco msg speak`, `caco scratch append`, and `caco agent reintegrate`) failed because an unrelated `projects['picasso'].friend_projects[0].permissions` entry contained invalid permission `checkout_write`. The operator fixed the config, but the failure mode was surprising because a picasso config issue blocked kittui lifecycle/status narration and scratch health-note reconciliation. Consider making config validation errors identify the owning config path/project and, if safe, isolate unrelated project operations from invalid friend-project permission entries. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-5c2b47 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Per-window macOS capture via CGWindowListCreateImage
description:
Extend QuartzServer with list_app_windows() returning every titled macOS app window (CGWindowID + bounds + owning PID + title), and capture_window(window_id) backed by CGWindowListCreateImage. Adds WindowSpec variants so a single QuartzServer can expose either the full display, one display, or one specific app window. Keeps the existing main-display path as the default. Tests verify the public API on the agent's macOS host.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete FFI ABI surface and generated header workflow
description: DESIGN.md defines a larger C ABI: runtime_configure, render_json, unplace, last_error, probe_json, bytes_free, abi_version_check, explicit byte lengths, cbindgen header snapshots, and ABI drift checks. Current kittui-ffi exposes runtime_new/free, place_json, string_free, abi_version, and a hand-maintained header/test scaffold. Implement the remaining ABI functions, preserve panic-safe boundaries, generate/update kittui.h through an xtask/cbindgen workflow, and add ABI snapshot validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: complete ffi abi surface and header workflow

title: Add kittui proof CLI command and Rust harness
description:
Add 'kittui proof' subcommand that, for each combination of (transport, animation on/off, unicode-placeholder vs absolute placement, single vs multi-placement, with/without subcell offset), prints labeled output of the exact escape bytes (hex + raw) and an optional --emit mode that prints them straight to the terminal so the operator can paste/run in Ghostty/kitty for visual verification. Mirror in Rust integration tests that snapshot the protocol bytes for each combination so 'cargo test -p kittui-cli proof' fails on any drift.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --pick-window: live Mac window picker that captures the chosen one
description: Add --pick-window flag to kitwm. On macOS with --features sck, lists CGWindowList::list_app_windows() in a kittui-rendered menu, lets the operator type to filter, arrows to navigate, Enter to surface that window as the capture target. Then runs the regular kitwm session against QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)). Visible new capability per overnight loop directive. Parent: bd-ed1c33.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-xquartz: spawn XQuartz in nolisten mode, attach via x11rb
description: New crate kittui-xquartz behind feature 'xquartz'. spawn() launches /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen $DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac or attaches to an existing :N. Implements XServer trait by delegating to kittui-xvfb's x11rb code path (extract that into a shared helper if needed). Detects XQuartz install at /opt/X11/bin or /Library/X11. Returns Unavailable if not found. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-xvfb: spawn Xvfb, claim display, SHM capture
description:
New crate kittui-xvfb that owns an Xvfb subprocess (or attaches to an existing :N display), connects via XCB, lists toplevel windows, and captures their framebuffer regions via MIT-SHM. Emits per-window (id, rect, rgba_bytes) snapshots and damage rectangles. Test exercises the spawn + connect path under a Linux runner and gracefully skips on macOS.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add batch/many render entrypoints that share resolved config and runtime state
description: Operator direction: common usage should support setting config/env once, then calling kittui many times or in a batch in that scope. Add well-documented batch entrypoints that resolve config once, reuse Runtime/cache/probe state, render/place many scenes efficiently, and expose this through CLI (e.g. JSONL or manifest input), Rust library, and FFI/TS binding surfaces. Ensure per-item overrides remain possible and tests verify repeated calls do not re-probe/re-upload unnecessarily.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Support placement_id (p=) and subcell offset (X=, Y=) on placement_command
description:
placement_command currently emits a=p,U=1,i=,c=,r= with no placement-id and no subcell pixel offset. The spec allows multiple placements of the same image via p= and subcell origin via X=/Y= for fractional positioning. Extend placement_command (or add a new builder) to accept placement_id and pixel offsets; thread through Runtime::place; expose via CLI.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Replace CGDisplayCreateImage with ScreenCaptureKit on macOS 12.3+
description: QuartzServer::capture currently uses CGDisplayCreateImage, which is soft-deprecated on macOS 14 and returns a black/dimmed frame on macOS 14/15/26 even when Screen Recording is granted. Operator reported black screen + ~1 FPS on macOS 26.4. Replace with ScreenCaptureKit (SCStream + SCStreamConfiguration) running on its own thread with latest-frame-wins, falling back to the legacy path only on macOS < 12.3. Add a non-black-frame assertion to the round-trip test. Affects both whole-display and per-window capture paths in CaptureTarget.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Expand kittui-wm from placeholder substrate to split/stack/tab layout semantics
description: DESIGN.md says kittui-wm currently reserves WindowTree/WindowGeometry but full split/stack/tab semantics and per-window scene generation should arrive after tmux showcase exercises diff-driven composition. Current crate description/source mark kittui-wm as a placeholder scaffold. Implement basic split, stack, and tab layout models, z-order handling, conversion to Composition entries, and tests for deterministic geometry output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Window picker: floating panel + Tab cycle to surface a Mac/X window
description: Inside the running kittui-wm session, hotkey (default Mod+Space) opens a kittui floating panel listing every window from QuartzServer::list_app_windows() + every X11 window across attached backends. Arrow keys navigate, Enter surfaces the window (calls QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)) and attaches as a new backend, or pins an existing one). Mod+W cycles surfaced windows. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Replace substring-based protocol tests with exact-grammar regressions
description:
kittui-kitty unit tests assert .contains(...) on small substrings, which passed the previous malformed leading-comma upload chunks for months. Convert each public function to use exact-grammar regression tests (golden strings for fixed inputs, structural parsers for the rest) covering: upload still single chunk, upload still multi-chunk, upload animation 3 frames + control, placement direct + tmux + unicode, placeholder grid with diacritics for a 4x3 footprint and an image id with non-zero top byte, delete by id, delete by placement-id. Each test must also fail if a stray comma or missing q= regresses.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: replace substring tests with grammar regressions

title: kittui-wm daemon: socket server + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY
description: New binary kittui-wm-daemon (or kittui-wm serve subcommand) that listens on a Unix socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock). Owns all backends + Pumps. Wire format: length-prefixed bincode. v1 messages: CmdListWindows, CmdTick, CmdInput(InputEvent), CmdResize { cols, rows }, CmdQuit. Daemon survives client disconnects. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: raw RGB transmission (kitty f=32) instead of PNG re-encode per frame
description: kittui_wm_demo currently spends most of each frame PNG-encoding a fresh capture. The kitty graphics spec supports raw RGB(A) transmission via f=24 (RGB) and f=32 (RGBA), which skips PNG entirely. Wire that through kittui-kitty's upload_still + upload_still_ex (already accepts UploadMedium::Direct{bytes}; just needs a CaptureFormat option and a code-path in Runtime::place to skip PNG). Should drop the demo's per-frame cost from ~60ms to ~10ms on a 320x192 capture.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
